title: As Winds That Blow Against A Star
content:
(For Aline)



     Now by what whim of wanton chance
        Do radiant eyes know sombre days?
     And feet that shod in light should dance
        Walk weary and laborious ways?

     But rays from Heaven, white and whole,
        May penetrate the gloom of earth;
     And tears but nourish, in your soul,
        The glory of celestial mirth.

     The darts of toil and sorrow, sent
        Against your peaceful beauty, are
     As foolish and as impotent
        As winds that blow against a star.
meaning:
Alfred Joyce Kilmer (Joyce)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "As Winds That Blow Against A Star"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
